message.content:
你是调研 finder。任务背景:MAformac 项目(纯端侧 macOS SwiftUI 演示助手,主演示面已定为 macOS,后续要做 UIUE 前置化)。
要求:
- 先用 ToolSearch 加载 WebSearch 和 WebFetch,然后联网搜索。
- 每条 findings 的 claim 必须来自你真实打开(WebFetch)或搜索摘要确认过的来源;禁止凭记忆编造 URL、数字、版本号。能 WebFetch 的关键来源尽量实访,并把 fetched 标 true。
- 优先 2024-2026 的新资料(Liquid Glass / macOS 26 时代),老经典(HIG 长期原则、Nielsen 启发式)也可收但注明。
- 输出全部用中文(URL/API 名/专有名词保留英文)。
- 8-15 条高质量 findings,宁缺毋滥。
调研角度:Apple 官方视角——macOS Human Interface Guidelines 最新结构与核心原则、Liquid Glass 设计语言(适用范围/迁移建议)、Apple Design Resources(Figma/Sketch 模板)、近两年 WWDC 中与 macOS App 设计/SwiftUI UI 开发相关的关键 session 要点。

attachment.content:
- archive-research-pack: Use when 一次多路 workflow 调研 / deep-research / pre-mortem oracle 搜证收口,需要把成果落盘防失忆。症状:调研完只想存一个总报告、transcript 在 /tmp 待清会丢、finder 引的 source URL 事后无法复核、各路一手档与综合报告混在一起。
- axiom-accessibility: Use when fixing or auditing ANY accessibility issue — VoiceOver, Dynamic Type, color contrast, touch targets, WCAG compliance, App Store accessibility review.
- axiom-ai: Use when implementing ANY Apple Intelligence or on-device AI feature. Covers Foundation Models, @Generable, LanguageModelSession, structured output, Tool protocol, iOS 26 AI integration.
- axiom-apple-docs: Use when ANY question involves Apple framework APIs, Swift compiler errors, or Xcode-bundled documentation. Covers Liquid Glass, Swift 6.2 concurrency, Foundation Models, SwiftData, StoreKit, 32 Swift compiler diagnostics.
- axiom-build: Use when ANY iOS build fails, test crashes, Xcode misbehaves, or environment issue occurs before debugging code. Covers build failures, compilation errors, dependency conflicts, simulator problems, environment-first diagnostics.
- axiom-concurrency: Use when writing ANY async code, actors, threads, or seeing ANY concurrency error. Covers Swift 6 concurrency, @MainActor, Sendable, data races, async/await patterns.
- axiom-data: Use when working with ANY data persistence, database, storage, CloudKit, migration, or serialization. Covers SwiftData, Core Data, GRDB, SQLite, CloudKit sync, file storage, Codable, migrations.
- axiom-design: Use when making design decisions, implementing HIG patterns, Liquid Glass, SF Symbols, typography, or structuring app entry points and authentication flows.
- axiom-games: Use when building ANY 2D or 3D game with SpriteKit, SceneKit, or RealityKit, or adding touch controls or game controller support. Covers scene graphs, ECS, physics, actions, game loops, rendering, TouchController, GCController.
- axiom-graphics: Use when working with ANY GPU rendering, Metal, OpenGL migration, shaders, 3D content, RealityKit, AR, USD/USDZ files, or display performance. Covers Metal migration, shader conversion, RealityKit ECS, RealityView, USDKit, ProMotion.
- axiom-health: Use when working with HealthKit, WorkoutKit, health data, workouts, or fitness features on iOS or watchOS. Covers permissions, queries, background delivery, custom workouts, multidevice coordination.
- axiom-integration: Use when integrating ANY iOS system feature - Siri, Shortcuts, widgets, IAP, localization, privacy, alarms, calendar, reminders, contacts, background tasks, push notifications, timers. Covers App Intents, WidgetKit, StoreKit, EventKit, Contacts.
- axiom-location: Use when implementing location services, maps, geofencing, or debugging location/MapKit issues. Covers Core Location, CLMonitor, MapKit, annotations, directions.
- axiom-macos: Use when building ANY macOS app — windows, menus, sandboxing, distribution, AppKit bridging or modernization (control events, state restoration, concentric corners), or macOS-specific SwiftUI patterns.
- axiom-media: Use when working with camera, photos, audio, haptics, ShazamKit, or Now Playing. Covers AVCaptureSession, PHPicker, PhotosPicker, AVFoundation, Core Haptics, audio recognition, MediaPlayer, CarPlay, MusicKit.
- axiom-networking: Use when implementing or debugging ANY network connection, API call, or socket. Covers URLSession, Network.framework, NetworkConnection, connection diagnostics.
- axiom-payments: Use when accepting ANY real-world payment — Apple Pay, Wallet passes, Tap to Pay, Orders in Wallet. NOT in-app purchase / digital content (use axiom-integration). Covers entitlements, certs, signing, App Review payment rules.
- axiom-performance: Use when app feels slow, memory grows, battery drains, or diagnosing ANY performance issue. Covers memory leaks, profiling, Instruments workflows, retain cycles, performance optimization.
- axiom-security: Use when storing credentials securely, encrypting data, implementing passkeys, securing AI/agentic features against prompt injection, code signing, or managing certificates and provisioning profiles.
- axiom-shipping: Use when preparing ANY app for submission, handling App Store rejections, writing appeals, or managing App Store Connect. Covers submission checklists, rejection troubleshooting, metadata requirements, privacy manifests, age ratings, export compliance.
- axiom-swift: Use when reviewing Swift code for modern idioms, working with noncopyable types, implementing drag and drop, adding debug deep links, or building for tvOS.
- axiom-swiftui: Use when building, fixing, or improving ANY SwiftUI UI — views, navigation, layout, animations, performance, architecture, gestures, debugging, iOS 26 features.
- axiom-testing: Use when writing ANY test, debugging flaky tests, making tests faster, or choosing Swift Testing vs XCTest. Covers unit tests, UI tests, async testing, test architecture.
- axiom-tools: Use when asking how to use Axiom or what skills exist, capturing console with xclog, symbolicating .ips/MetricKit/.crash crashes with xcsym, driving/validating simulator UI & accessibility with xcui, or analyzing xctrace/CPU profiles with xcprof.
- axiom-uikit: Use when bridging UIKit and SwiftUI, modernizing UIKit apps (scene lifecycle, resizability), debugging Auto Layout, Combine, TextKit, PencilKit, or UIKit animations.
- axiom-vision: Use when implementing ANY computer vision feature — image analysis, pose detection, person segmentation, subject lifting, text recognition, barcode scanning.
- axiom-watchos: Use when building ANY watchOS app — app structure, independent apps, Watch Connectivity, Smart Stack widgets, complications, controls, RelevanceKit, background tasks, ClockKit migration.
- axiom-xcode-mcp: Use when connecting to Xcode via MCP, using xcrun mcpbridge, or working with ANY Xcode MCP tool (XcodeRead, BuildProject, RunTests, RenderPreview). Covers setup, tool reference, workflow patterns, troubleshooting.
- closeout-receipt-writer: Use when 一个 apply/重型任务/审计 wave 收口需要写 receipt 证据。症状:想用 hardcoded pass、读 metadata flag 自证 verdict、凭印象写状态、或 receipt 与实跑 log 脱节;0-34 灾难根因正是 receipt 读自己的 metadata flag 翻 pass、零 token 证据。
- doc-cascade-sweep: Use when 范式翻案/架构大改/口径回写后,grep 全仓命中几十个文件含过期锚点或旧数字,需要全仓级联更新。症状:看到 grep 命中 60+ 文件就想 sed 全量替换、或一个决策只 append 进一个文件没回写文档组、或改了带日期的历史快照正文破坏一手溯源。
- ios-debugger-agent: Build, run, and debug iOS apps on Simulator with XcodeBuildMCP. Use when launching an app, inspecting simulator UI or logs, or diagnosing runtime behavior.
- ios-ettrace-performance: Capture and interpret iOS Simulator ETTrace profiles. Use when profiling launch or runtime latency, comparing traces, or finding CPU-heavy stacks.
- ios-simulator-skill: 29 production-ready scripts for iOS app testing, building, and automation. Provides semantic UI navigation, build automation, accessibility testing, and simulator lifecycle management. Optimized for AI agents with minimal token output.
- openspec-apply-change: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change. Use when the user wants to start implementing, continue implementation, or work through tasks.
- openspec-archive-change: Archive a completed change in the experimental workflow. Use when the user wants to finalize and archive a change after implementation is complete.
- openspec-explore: Enter explore mode - a thinking partner for exploring ideas, investigating problems, and clarifying requirements. Use when the user wants to think through something before or during a change.
- openspec-propose: Propose a new change with all artifacts generated in one step. Use when the user wants to quickly describe what they want to build and get a complete proposal with design, specs, and tasks ready for implementation.
- openspec-sync-specs: Sync delta specs from a change to main specs. Use when the user wants to update main specs with changes from a delta spec, without archiving the change.
- verify-external-claims: Use when 一个多路 workflow/finder 调研、deep-research、或综合官报告引用了 arxiv ID、精确数字（调用数/百分比/star/benchmark）、或 repo 活跃度断言,且这些要驱动决策。症状:finder 在搜证压力下高发编造精确数字 + 引用 ID,且常安到真实存在的 source 上(issue/论文真、数字假),综合官即使做了本机 grep 也漏外部声称。
- opsx:apply: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change (Experimental)
- opsx:archive: Archive a completed change in the experimental workflow
- opsx:explore: Enter explore mode - think through ideas, investigate problems, clarify requirements
- opsx:propose: Propose a new change - create it and generate all artifacts in one step
- opsx:sync: Sync delta specs from a change to main specs
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"Get to know the new design system - WWDC25 - Videos - Apple Developer","url":"https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/356/"},{"title":"Meet Liquid Glass - WWDC25 - Videos - Apple Developer","url":"https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/219/"},{"title":"Liquid Glass | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/documentation/TechnologyOverviews/liquid-glass"},{"title":"How Apple Designs Their UI: An Apple Design System Breakdown (2026) | Superdesign","url":"https://superdesign.dev/blog/apple-design-system"},{"title":"iOS 26 Liquid Glass: Comprehensive Swift/SwiftUI Reference","url":"https://medium.com/@madebyluddy/overview-37b3685227aa"},{"title":"Liquid Glass - Wikipedia","url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liquid_Glass"},{"title":"Materials | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/materials"},{"title":"Apple’s New Liquid Glass Design: Practical Guidance for Designers — designed for humans","url":"https://designedforhumans.tech/blog/liquid-glass-smart-or-bad-for-accessibility"},{"title":"Human Interface Guidelines | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/"},{"title":"Apple Liquid Glass design in iOS 26: Redesign your app icon | MobileAction","url":"https://www.mobileaction.co/blog/apple-liquid-glass-design/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Apple's macOS Human Interface Guidelines and Liquid Glass in 2025:

## Overview

Liquid Glass is a design language developed by Apple announced on June 9, 2025, at the Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC). It was introduced in iOS 26, iPadOS 26, macOS Tahoe, tvOS 26, visionOS 26, and watchOS 26.

## What is Liquid Glass?

Liquid Glass is a new digital meta-material that dynamically bends and shapes light, behaving and moving organically in a manner that feels more like a lightweight liquid, responding to both the fluidity of touch and the dynamism of modern apps. It is composed of multiple layers that continuously shift tint, shadows, and dynamic range to ensure legibility while maintaining visual clarity.

## Updates to Human Interface Guidelines

Apple published updates to the Human Interface Guidelines along with new UI kits on Apple Design Resources. Apple's updated human interface guidelines say that apps made with Liquid Glass should show hierarchy between content and controls.

## macOS-Specific Changes

On macOS, Mini, Small, and Medium controls continue using rounded rectangles for compact, high-density layouts, while Large controls now use capsule shapes, alongside a new X-Large size, which takes advantage of Liquid Glass to provide emphasis in more spacious areas.

Human Interface Guidelines resources are available at the Apple Developer documentation website for implementing these design principles.

I've completed the web search for "Apple Liquid Glass design language WWDC 2025". Here are the key findings:

## Overview

Liquid Glass is a design language developed by Apple as a unified visual theme for the graphical user interfaces for its suite of operating systems, announced on June 9, 2025, at the Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC).

## Core Design Principles

Liquid Glass delivers a unified design language across all platforms, seamlessly blending hardware and software. Rather than trying to simply recreate a material from the physical world, Liquid Glass is a new digital meta-material that dynamically bends and shapes light.

## Key Features

This translucent material reflects and refracts its surroundings, while dynamically transforming to help bring greater focus to content, delivering a new level of vitality across controls, navigation, app icons, widgets, and more.

## Platform Coverage

The new design extends across platforms — iOS 26, iPadOS 26, macOS Tahoe 26, watchOS 26, and tvOS 26 — to establish even more harmony while maintaining the distinct qualities that make each unique.

## Design Evolution

Liquid Glass marks the most extensive software design update Apple has made, reshaping the relationship between interface and content through a brand new set of heuristics.

input.prompt: Extract exact details about Liquid Glass: what it is, which platforms/OS versions it covers, what UI elements it applies to (controls, navigation, app icons, widgets), design intent, and any specifics about macOS Tahoe. Include exact naming and version numbers.

input.query: WWDC25 session 356 "Get to know the new design system" macOS Liquid Glass key points

content.content:
# Liquid Glass: Technical Overview

## What It Is
Liquid Glass is Apple's new translucent material that "combines the optical qualities of glass with a fluidity only Apple can achieve." It uses real-time rendering and dynamically reacts to movement with specular highlights, intelligently adapting its color based on surrounding content and environment brightness.

## Platform Coverage
The design extends across five operating systems:
- iOS 26
- iPadOS 26
- macOS Tahoe 26
- watchOS 26
- tvOS 26

## UI Elements Enhanced
Liquid Glass applies to:
- **Small controls**: buttons, switches, sliders, text, media controls
- **Navigation**: tab bars, sidebars
- **System experiences**: Lock Screen, Home Screen, notifications, Control Center
- **Home Screen/Desktop**: Dock, app icons, widgets (multiple layers)

## Design Intent
The material is designed to "bring greater focus to content" while maintaining familiarity. It "dynamically transforms to help bring greater focus to content, delivering a new level of vitality across controls, navigation, app icons, widgets, and more."

## macOS Tahoe 26 Specifics
- Transparent menu bar for expanded display perception
- Customizable desktop and Dock with widgets and icons
- Multiple appearance options: light, dark, colorful tinted versions, and an "elegant new clear look"
- Controls adapt to light or dark environments with different visual treatments

Web search results for query: "WWDC25 session 356 "Get to know the new design system" macOS Liquid Glass key points"

Links: [{"title":"Get to know the new design system - WWDC25 - Videos - Apple Developer","url":"https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/356/"},{"title":"Meet Liquid Glass - WWDC25 - Videos - Apple Developer","url":"https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/219/"},{"title":"Build an AppKit app with the new design - WWDC25 - Videos - Apple Developer","url":"https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/"},{"title":"Liquid Glass - Wikipedia","url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liquid_Glass"},{"title":"Apple WWDC25 Recap: Apple Intelligence, Liquid Glass, and Next-Gen OS 🚀 - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/om_shree_0709/apple-wwdc25-recap-apple-intelligence-liquid-glass-and-next-gen-os-2inn"},{"title":"Apple WWDC25 Recap: Apple Intelligence, Liquid Glass, and Next-Gen OS 🚀 | by Om Shree | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@omshree0709/apple-wwdc25-recap-apple-intelligence-liquid-glass-and-next-gen-os-38152a5d4adf"},{"title":"Apple introduces a delightful and elegant new software design - Apple","url":"https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2025/06/apple-introduces-a-delightful-and-elegant-new-software-design/"},{"title":"Hyper Pixel | Apple’s Liquid Glass: Why WWDC25 Just Changed the Future of App Design","url":"https://hyperpixel.co.uk/insights/apples-liquid-glass-why-wwdc25-just-changed-the-future-of-app-design/"},{"title":"Build a SwiftUI app with the new design - WWDC25 - Videos - Apple Developer","url":"https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/323/"},{"title":"WWDC25: Meet Liquid Glass | Apple - YouTube","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IrGYUq1mklk"}]

Based on the search results, here are the key points from WWDC25 Session 356 "Get to know the new design system" about macOS Liquid Glass:

## Visual Design & System Colors

The family of system colors has been adjusted in subtle but meaningful ways across Light, Dark and Increased Contrast appearances to work in harmony with Liquid Glass, improving hue differentiation while maintaining the optimistic spirit that makes them uniquely Apple.

Typography has been refined to strengthen clarity and structure, now bolder and left-aligned to improve readability in key moments like alerts and onboarding.

## Control Shapes & Sizes

On macOS, Mini, Small, and Medium controls will continue using rounded rectangles for compact, high-density layouts like inspector panels, while Large controls will now use capsule shapes, alongside a new X-Large size, which takes advantage of Liquid Glass to provide emphasis in more spacious areas.

## Information Architecture & Content Focus

As apps become more immersive and content-focused, the UI should support interaction where needed and remain unobtrusive when it's not. Liquid Glass defines a new functional layer in the UI, floating above content to bring structure and clarity without stealing focus.

Action Sheets now spring from the action itself, serving as the source for the action sheet, with clearly defined roles anchoring the interaction as it extends directly from the tapped element.

## Design Philosophy

Liquid Glass marks the most extensive software design update, reshaping the relationship between interface and content through a brand new set of heuristics, resulting in a harmonized design language that's more cohesive, adaptive and expressive.

Based on the search results for WWDC25 Session 323 "Build a SwiftUI app with the new design," here are the key takeaways regarding the glassEffect API:

## Core glassEffect APIs

To add glass to custom views, use the glassEffect modifier. By default, a glass effect will be applied within a capsule shape behind your content. Use glassEffect to add Liquid Glass visuals to custom views, with vibrant adaptive text and tint colors.

## Customization Options

Customize the shape of the glass effect by providing a shape to the modifier. For especially important views, use a tint modifier. Similar to toolbar buttons, only use this to convey meaning and not just for visual effect.

On iOS, for custom controls or for containers with interactive elements, add the interactive modifier to the glass effect. Glass reacts to user interaction by scaling, bouncing, and shimmering, matching the effect provided by toolbar buttons and sliders.

## Grouping Multiple Glass Elements

To combine multiple glass elements, use the GlassEffectContainer. This grouping is essential for visual correctness. Glass can not sample other glass, so having nearby glass elements in different containers will result in inconsistent behavior. Using a glass container allows these elements to share their sampling region, providing a consistent visual result.

## Animated Transitions

Add these transitions to your own glass container by using the glassEffectID modifier. To configure this, I first declare a local namespace. Then, I associate the namespace with each of the glassEffect elements in my expanded stack of badges and with my toolbar button.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Key Design Principles from WWDC25 - New Design System

## Concentric Corners/Corner Radius

The system uses **three shape types to build concentric layouts**:

1. **Fixed shapes** - Have a constant corner radius
2. **Capsules** - Use a radius that's half the height of the container
3. **Concentric shapes** - Calculate their radius by subtracting padding from the parent's

**Key principle**: "By aligning radii and margins around a shared center, shapes can comfortably nest within each other." Views are "mathematically centered when it makes sense— and subtly offset when it doesn't."

The capsule shape appears throughout the system "in the mirrored proportions of sliders and switches, and echoed in bars, buttons, and the rounded corners of grouped table views."

## Control Grouping

- **Items grouped using the correct API will automatically update to share a background and preserve their spatial relationship**
- Group bar items **by function and frequency**
- **Do not group symbols with text** (e.g., "Select" with a share icon) as it could be perceived as a single button
- Text buttons should sit on their own containers
- **Primary actions** (like Done) stay separate and appear tinted

## Functional/Navigation Layer

Liquid Glass "defines a new functional layer in the UI, floating above your content to bring structure and clarity, without ever stealing focus."

**Key principle**: "As apps become more immersive and content-focused, the UI should support interaction where needed, and remain unobtrusive when it's not."

Action Sheets now demonstrate this: "It used to appear at the bottom of the screen, no matter where the action came from. Now, it springs from the action itself, which serves as the source for the action sheet."

## macOS Control Sizes

- **Mini, Small, and Medium controls** continue using rounded rectangles for "compact, high-density layouts like inspector panels"
- **Large controls** now use capsule shapes
- **X-Large** is a new size that "takes advantage of Liquid Glass to provide emphasis in more spacious areas"

"Used together, they create hierarchy and balance across complex desktop layouts."

## Typography Changes

"Typography has been refined to strengthen clarity and structure, now **bolder and left-aligned** to improve readability in key moments like alerts and onboarding."

## Color System Changes

"Our family of system colors has been adjusted in subtle but meaningful ways across Light, Dark and Increased Contrast appearances, so that they work in harmony with Liquid Glass, **improving hue differentiation, without losing the optimistic spirit that makes them uniquely Apple**."

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Apple Design Resources for 2025:

## Official Apple Design Resources

Apple offers official design templates, icon production templates, color guides, and more for designing apps accurately and quickly. Apple design kits for Figma and Sketch are now available for iOS, iPadOS, and macOS 27.

## iOS 26, iPadOS 26, and macOS Tahoe 26 Resources

The updated files, available on the Apple Design Resources website, include iOS 26, iPadOS 26 and macOS Tahoe 26 Sketch Library and App Icon Templates (available for Sketch, Photoshop, and Illustrator).

## Liquid Glass UI Kit

Icon Composer lets you create layered icons out of Liquid Glass from a single design for iPhone, iPad, Mac, and Apple Watch. Updates to Liquid Glass include expanded component and state support, naming changes to better align with code, improved resizing, and the addition of Dark Mode for macOS.

## How to Download

The official resources can be accessed at **https://developer.apple.com/design/resources/**, where you can download the Figma and Sketch design kits. Resources are available for Figma, Sketch, Adobe XD, and as SwiftUI/React components.

input.prompt: What does this Apple Developer news say about design kits for iOS, iPadOS, and macOS 27? Extract the date, what tools (Figma/Sketch), what's new, Icon Composer, and any Liquid Glass updates. Give exact wording and version numbers.

Extract SwiftUI Liquid Glass API guidance: toolbar changes, ToolbarSpacer, tabViewBottomAccessory, search role placement, glassEffect, GlassEffectContainer, glassEffectID, interactive modifier, backgroundExtensionEffect, scrollEdgeEffect. Give exact API names and the rules for when to use each.

content.content:
# Design Kits for iOS, iPadOS, and macOS 27

**Date:** June 23, 2026

**Tools:** Figma and Sketch

**What's New:**
- Updates to Liquid Glass
- Expanded component and state support
- Naming changes to better align with code
- Improved resizing
- The addition of Dark Mode for macOS

**Icon Composer:** Not mentioned in this article

**Liquid Glass Updates:** "Updates to Liquid Glass" are included (exact details not provided beyond this mention)

**Access:** Design kits are available for download from the [Apple Design Resources](https://developer.apple.com/design/resources/)

# SwiftUI Liquid Glass API Guidance

## Toolbar Changes
Toolbar items now "automatically placed in Liquid Glass and grouped." Use `ToolbarSpacer(.fixed)` to separate groups and `ToolbarSpacer(.flexible)` to expand space between bottom items. Hide the glass container with `.sharedBackgroundVisibility(.hidden)`.

## Tab Bar APIs
- **`.tabBarMinimizeBehavior()`**: Minimizes the tab bar on scroll with `.onScrollDown` option
- **`.tabViewBottomAccessory()`**: Adds a view above the tab bar
- **`@Environment(\.tabViewBottomAccessoryPlacement)`**: Detects if the accessory is inline (minimized) or full size

## Search Placement
On iPhone, search appears at the bottom; on iPad/Mac, top trailing. Apply `.searchable()` on `NavigationSplitView` for dedicated search page behavior. Use `Tab(role: .search)` for tab bar integration.

## Glass Effect APIs
- **`.glassEffect()`**: "Manually add Liquid Glass to any view" with capsule shape by default. Supports `.interactive()` modifier for interactivity and `.tint()` for colors.
- **`GlassEffectContainer`**: "Wrap multiple glass elements" to enable proper transitions and morphing
- **`.glassEffectID()`**: Combined with `@Namespace`, enables transition morphing between glass views

## Background & Scroll Effects
- **`.backgroundExtensionEffect()`**: "Mirrors and blurs images/content" behind floating sidebars
- **`.scrollEdgeEffectStyle(.hard)`**: Creates sharp edge effects on scrollable content

I found the WWDC25 session 310 "Build an AppKit app with the new design" with information about NSGlassEffectView and key macOS changes.

## Key Findings:

**NSGlassEffectView API:**
To place content on glass, use the NSGlassEffectView API. Setting a contentView allows AppKit to apply all of the necessary visual treatments to keep your content legible as the glass adapts to its surroundings. You can customize the appearance of the glass using the corner radius and tint color properties.

**Key macOS Design Changes:**
Key changes include tab views, split views, bars, presentations, search, and controls, and how to use Liquid Glass in custom UI. A key element of this new design is the Liquid Glass material, a translucent surface that reflects and refracts light, creating a sense of depth and dynamism within the user interface.

**Toolbar Changes:**
In the new design system, toolbar elements are placed on a glass material, and the entire toolbar appears to float above the content, enhancing the sense of hierarchy within the window.

**Grouping Glass Elements:**
When multiple glass shapes are close together, group them by using 'NSGlassEffectContainerView' to ensure fluid visual effects, uniform appearance, and consistent sampling of nearby content.

You can access the full session at the Apple Developer website: https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Apple HIG and designing for macOS:

## Core Principles

Apple's Human Interface Guidelines are built on several core principles: Clarity (interfaces should be legible, precise, and easy to understand), Deference (the UI helps users focus on their content and tasks by minimizing unnecessary visual clutter), and Depth (visual layers and realistic motion convey hierarchy and facilitate understanding).

## macOS-Specific Design Considerations

People rely on the power, spaciousness, and flexibility of a Mac as they perform in-depth productivity tasks, view media or content, and play games, often using several apps at once.

For macOS, the focus is on pointer and keyboard usage, dense information display is acceptable, menu bars, toolbars, and keyboard shortcuts should be used extensively, and windows are resizable with precise control.

## Menus, Toolbars, and Windows

Practical uses of macOS guidelines include defining menu structures and Command-key shortcuts, choosing between SwiftUI Toolbar and NSToolbar patterns, and implementing resizable and full-screen window behaviors.

For toolbars specifically, remove background colors from custom toolbars and tab bars, rely on layout and grouping to express hierarchy rather than unnecessary decoration, organize bar items logically by function and frequency of use, and use tinting to help primary actions stand out clearly.

## Updated Design Language (2025)

On macOS, Mini, Small, and Medium controls will continue using rounded rectangles for compact, high-density layouts like inspector panels, while Large controls will now use capsule shapes alongside the new X-Large size, which takes advantage of Liquid Glass to provide emphasis in more spacious areas.

content.text: Let me fetch the AppKit notes for detail and the "designing for macOS" HIG page via a summarizer source.

input.prompt: Extract macOS/AppKit new-design guidance: NSGlassEffectView, NSGlassEffectContainerView, toolbar floating over content, scroll edge effects, split view / sidebar changes, tinting rules, and any advice on not over-using glass. Give exact API names and rules.

This is "Meet Liquid Glass". Extract: the two variants of Liquid Glass (Regular vs Clear) and when to use each, the two layers (highlights/shadow), guidance on using it sparingly / only in the navigation layer, concentric design, and adaptivity/accessibility. Give exact phrasing.

content.content:
# macOS AppKit New Design Guidance

## Glass Effect Views

**NSGlassEffectView** is the primary API for applying glass to custom UI:
- Place content in the `contentView` property
- Customize appearance via `cornerRadius` and `tintColor` properties
- Use **NSGlassEffectContainerView** to group multiple glass shapes in close proximity to avoid visual artifacts and improve performance
- Grouped containers share adaptive appearance automatically

## Toolbar Behavior

Toolbars now "float over content" with intelligent grouping:
- Items automatically group based on control type, rendered on a single glass layer
- Override grouping using explicit `NSToolbarItemGroup` or spacers
- Material adapts to background brightness, switching light/dark automatically
- Remove glass from non-interactive items by setting `isBordered` to `false`
- Apply tinting with `style` property (`.prominent` value) and `backgroundTintColor`

## Sidebar & Split View Changes

Sidebars appear as floating glass panes above content:
- Legacy `NSVisualEffectView` is unnecessary—remove it
- Enable content to extend beneath sidebars by setting `automaticallyAdjustsSafeAreaInsets` to `true` on split items
- Use **NSBackgroundExtensionView** when avoiding content overlap is preferable; it mirrors and blurs content while extending appearance edge-to-edge
- Inspectors use edge-to-edge glass alongside content

## Scroll Edge Effects

Two styles available:
- **Soft-edge** uses progressive/variable blur
- **Hard-edge** uses more opaque backing
- Automatically applied beneath floating content via **NSSplitViewItemAccessoryViewController**

## Control Sizing & Prominence

New standard sizes: mini, small, medium, large, plus **extra large** for prominent actions. Controls are larger overall; use Auto Layout instead of hard-coded heights. Configure prominence levels via `NSTintProminence` enum and `tintProminence` property.

## Avoiding Over-Use

Use `NSView.prefersCompactControlSizeMetrics` for compatibility escape hatch when new metrics aren't desired.

# Liquid Glass: Key Components & Usage Guidelines

## Two Variants

**Regular**
- "The most versatile and the one you will be using the most"
- "This variant gives you all the visual and adaptive effects we've talked about, and provides legibility regardless of context. It works in any size, over any content and anything can be placed on top of it."
- Use in any context

**Clear**
- "Does not have adaptive behaviors. It is permanently more transparent, which allows the richness of the content underneath to come through and interact with the glass in beautiful ways."
- "To provide enough legibility for symbols or labels, it needs a dimming layer to darken the underlying content."
- Use only when three conditions are met:
  1. "The element you're applying it to is over media-rich content"
  2. "Your content layer won't be negatively affected by introducing a dimming layer"
  3. "The content sitting above it is bold and bright"

**Critical:** "They should never be mixed, as they each have their own characteristics and specific use cases."

## Two Key Layers

**Highlights Layer**
- "Light sources inside of this environment shine on the material producing highlights that respond to geometry"
- "On interactions, such as locking and unlocking your phone, these lights move in space, causing light to travel around the material, defining its silhouette"

**Shadows Layer**
- "The element is aware of what's behind it and increases the opacity of its shadow when it is over text. Conversely, it lowers the opacity of its shadow when it is over a solid light background."

## Sparse Usage & Navigation Layer

"You may be tempted to use Liquid Glass everywhere but **it is best reserved for the navigation layer that floats above the content of your app.**"

**Avoid:**
- Content layer: "Making it Liquid Glass would make it compete with other elements and muddy the hierarchy. So keep it in the content layer instead to ensure clarity"
- Glass-on-glass: "Stacking Liquid Glass elements on top of each other can quickly make the interface feel cluttered and confusing."

## Concentric Design

"Glass controls **nest perfectly into the rounded corners of windows, maintaining concentricity throughout the UI.**"

## Adaptivity & Accessibility

**Adaptivity:**
- "Liquid Glass is designed from the ground up to be adaptive to both its size and its environment"
- Elements automatically "flip from light to dark based on the background"
- "When Liquid Glass flexes and morphs to larger sizes...it simulates a thicker, more substantial material. It casts deeper, richer shadows, has more pronounced lensing and refraction effects, and a softer scattering of light"

**Accessibility Features:**
- "Reduced Transparency, makes Liquid Glass frostier and obscures more of the content behind it"
- "Increased contrast, makes elements predominantly black or white and highlights them with a contrasting border"
- "Reduced Motion decreases the intensity of some effects and disables any elastic properties for the material"
- "These are available automatically whenever you use the new material"

Download Apple's new Icon Composer tool","url":"https://www.idownloadblog.com/2025/06/11/download-apple-icon-composer-create-liquid-glass-icons/"},{"title":"Creating your app icon using Icon Composer | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/documentation/Xcode/creating-your-app-icon-using-icon-composer"},{"title":"Apple's new Icon composer for liquid glass UI","url":"https://designzig.com/apples-new-icon-composer-for-liquid-glass-ui/"},{"title":"Create icons with Icon Composer - WWDC25 - Videos - Apple Developer","url":"https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/361/"},{"title":"Apple Design Resources - Apple Developer","url":"https://developer.apple.com/design/resources/"},{"title":"Crafting Liquid Glass app icons with Icon Composer","url":"https://www.createwithswift.com/crafting-liquid-glass-app-icons-with-icon-composer/"},{"title":"Liquid Glass App Icons: iOS 26 Icon Composer Guide | IconikAI","url":"https://www.iconikai.com/blog/liquid-glass-app-icon-icon-composer-2026"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Apple's Icon Composer app and its capabilities with Liquid Glass for macOS 2025:

## Icon Composer Overview

Icon Composer lets you create layered icons out of Liquid Glass from a single design for iPhone, iPad, Mac, and Apple Watch. macOS Tahoe 26 lets users customize the desktop and Dock with new looks for app icons and widgets crafted from multiple layers of Liquid Glass.

## Layered Design & Liquid Glass Features

You can import and organize your artwork layers, then adjust properties including specular highlights, refraction, translucency, and shadows to give your icon material presence. By default, it'll always be one layer, but you can go all the way up to four. They found this number provides the right bounds for how much visual complexity an icon should have.

## Default Icon Shape

iOS 26 slightly rounds the rectangle and applies its mask automatically, so anything pushed to the corners risks getting clipped. This indicates that the default enclosure shape for app icons in macOS 2025 is indeed a rounded rectangle.

## Additional Capabilities

You can easily adjust Liquid Glass properties, preview with dynamic lighting effects and annotate across appearance modes. The appearance modes were renamed to default, dark and mono, with the artwork producing all the appearances for clear and for tinted.

KG | Blog - iOS 26 in detail: Liquid Glass UI between Usability and Accessibility","url":"https://letsdev.de/en/blog/ios-26-in-detail-liquid-glass-ui-between-usability-and-accessibility.php"},{"title":"Adopting Apple's Liquid Glass: Examples and best practices - LogRocket Blog","url":"https://blog.logrocket.com/ux-design/adopting-liquid-glass-examples-best-practices/"},{"title":"Apple Liquid Glass design in iOS 26: Redesign your app icon | MobileAction","url":"https://www.mobileaction.co/blog/apple-liquid-glass-design/"},{"title":"Apple’s New Design Language: Liquid Glass (Analyse) | by Özkan Bırak | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@ozkanbirak/apples-new-design-language-liquid-glass-analyse-9d61eb8d23fb"}]

I found relevant information about Apple's Liquid Glass design and the HIG guidance. Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding the "Adopting Liquid Glass" article with checklist testing and reduced transparency toolbar tint:

Liquid Glass adapts color and contrast to underlying content and honors system settings for Reduced Transparency, Reduced Motion and High Contrast. The Apple HIG documentation emphasizes accessibility compliance for the new design system.

Respect system toggles: Reduce Transparency must replace glass with solid colour; Reduce Motion disables parallax highlights. This is a key requirement when testing Liquid Glass implementations.

The search results point to the official Apple Developer Documentation for "Adopting Liquid Glass," which provides detailed guidance on implementing this material in apps. Apple's HIG update and independent audits converge on four rules of thumb: Minimum contrast: ensure 4.5:1 text/background ratio after blur.

Ensure tooltips remain visible on solid backgrounds when transparency is off. This is part of the testing checklist to ensure your interface works correctly with accessibility features enabled.

The official Apple documentation at developer.apple.com/documentation/technologyoverviews/adopting-liquid-glass provides the authoritative guidance on this topic, though the search results only show a brief preview of that page.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Let me fetch the "Adopting Liquid Glass" HIG article and the createwithswift design principles piece for concrete accessibility/adoption checklist.

input.prompt: Summarize Apple's three principles for the new design system: Hierarchy, Harmony, Consistency. What does each mean per Apple's guidance? Also extract any concrete rules about content layer vs navigation layer, concentricity, and using standard components. Give exact phrasing where possible.

content.content:
# Apple's Liquid Glass Design System: Three Core Principles

## Hierarchy
**Definition:** "Establish a clear visual hierarchy where controls and interface elements elevate and distinguish the content beneath them."

In practice, the interface now adapts dynamically to content and user context. Navigation and layout adjust in real-time rather than using fixed patterns, allowing users to focus on what matters most.

## Harmony
**Definition:** "Align with the concentric design of the hardware and software to create harmony between interface elements, system experiences, and devices."

This principle emphasizes aligning software with each device's physical reality. Glass materials blend the interface seamlessly into the app and device itself, extending across multitasking and multiple platforms without forcing uniformity.

## Consistency
**Definition:** "Adopt platform conventions to maintain a consistent design that continuously adapts across window sizes and displays."

Design remains predictable and reduces cognitive load while stretching across different contexts. Elements adapt fluidly—exemplified by iPadOS now resembling macOS—while maintaining recognizable interaction patterns.

## Key Content Rules
- Navigation and controls should not compete with or obscure content
- The interface is now "fully content-aware," prioritizing, hiding, or simplifying components based on user actions
- Related actions are grouped intelligently to create a more guided experience
- Materials and motion serve functional purposes (signaling depth, feedback, context changes) rather than decoration

content.text: I have enough high-quality material. Compiling findings.

input.angle: Apple 官方视角:macOS HIG 最新结构与核心原则 + Liquid Glass 设计语言(适用范围/迁移) + Apple Design Resources 模板 + 2024-2026 WWDC 中 macOS App 设计/SwiftUI 关键 session 要点
input.summary: Apple 于 2025 年 6 月 WWDC25 发布 Liquid Glass,是覆盖 iOS 26 / iPadOS 26 / macOS Tahoe 26 / watchOS 26 / tvOS 26 五平台的统一设计语言,也是 Apple 迄今最大规模的软件设计更新。核心思想:Liquid Glass 是一层"浮在内容之上的功能性导航层",用半透明动态材质带来结构与层级,但不抢内容焦点。官方最重要的落地纪律是"用得克制"——只用在导航层,不要放进内容层,不要 glass 叠 glass。新设计系统的三大原则被 Apple 表述为 Hierarchy(层级)、Harmony(和谐/与硬件同心圆)、Consistency(一致/跨窗口尺寸自适应),仍建立在 HIG 长期的 Clarity/Deference/Depth 之上。macOS 侧有具体变化:控件尺寸新增 X-Large、Large 改胶囊形、Mini/Small/Medium 保留圆角矩形用于高密度检查器面板;工具栏浮于内容之上并按功能自动分组;系统色与排版为配合 Liquid Glass 做了调整(更粗、左对齐)。工程侧,SwiftUI 提供 glassEffect / GlassEffectContainer / glassEffectID / ToolbarSpacer 等 API,AppKit 提供 NSGlassEffectView / NSGlassEffectContainerView;标准组件自动获得 Liquid Glass 与无障碍适配(Reduce Transparency / Increased Contrast / Reduce Motion)。Apple Design Resources 已更新 Figma/Sketch 模板(2026-06 又发布 macOS 27 版含 macOS Dark Mode),配套 Icon Composer 制作最多 4 层的 Liquid Glass 分层图标。对 MAformac 这类纯端侧 macOS SwiftUI 演示助手,官方结论是:优先用标准组件让系统自动上 Liquid Glass,把 glass 留给导航/工具层,内容卡片保持清晰,并务必在打开无障碍开关下验证可读性。

findings.claim: Liquid Glass 是 Apple 在 2025-06-09 WWDC25 发布的统一设计语言,覆盖 iOS 26 / iPadOS 26 / macOS Tahoe 26 / watchOS 26 / tvOS 26 五个平台,是 Apple 迄今最大规模的软件设计更新,作用于 controls、navigation、app icons、widgets 等。
findings.source_title: Apple introduces a delightful and elegant new software design — Apple Newsroom
findings.notes: 官方 newsroom。macOS Tahoe 26 特有:透明菜单栏、可自定义桌面/Dock、light/dark/tinted/clear 多种外观。

findings.claim: 官方核心纪律:Liquid Glass 最好只保留给'浮在内容之上的导航层(navigation layer)',不要到处用;不要把内容层做成 Liquid Glass(会与其他元素争抢、搞乱层级),也不要 glass 叠 glass(界面会显得杂乱混乱)。
findings.notes: 原文:'it is best reserved for the navigation layer that floats above the content of your app';明确点名 content layer 与 glass-on-glass 反例。

findings.claim: Liquid Glass 有两种变体且绝不可混用:Regular(最通用、自适应、任何尺寸/任何内容上都保证可读,默认首选)与 Clear(永久更透明、无自适应、需加 dimming layer,仅当'覆盖媒体丰富内容 + 内容层不受变暗影响 + 上方元素粗且亮'三条件同时满足时才用)。
findings.notes: 原文 'They should never be mixed'。对 demo:默认用 Regular 即可。

findings.claim: 新设计系统的三大原则被 Apple 表述为 Hierarchy(控件抬升并衬托其下内容)、Harmony(与硬件的同心圆设计对齐,界面与设备融为一体)、Consistency(采用平台惯例,随窗口尺寸/显示器持续自适应);仍建立在 HIG 长期的 Clarity/Deference/Depth 之上。
findings.source_title: Liquid Glass: Redefining design through Hierarchy, Harmony and Consistency — Create with Swift
findings.notes: 三原则引自 Apple HIG 新页面;Clarity/Deference/Depth 为 HIG 长期基础原则(见 designing-for-macos)。

findings.claim: macOS 控件尺寸变化:Mini/Small/Medium 继续用圆角矩形(适合检查器面板等高密度紧凑布局),Large 改为胶囊形,并新增 X-Large 尺寸利用 Liquid Glass 在更宽敞区域提供强调;组合使用以在复杂桌面布局中建立层级与平衡。
findings.source_title: Get to know the new design system — WWDC25 Session 356
findings.notes: macOS 专属规则,对 MAformac 桌面卡片/工具区选控件尺寸直接相关。

findings.claim: 同心圆(concentric)设计:系统用三种形状构建嵌套布局——fixed(固定半径)、capsule(半径=容器高度一半)、concentric(半径=父容器半径减内边距);玻璃控件应完美嵌入窗口圆角,全程保持同心。排版改为更粗、左对齐以提升 alert/onboarding 可读性;系统色跨 Light/Dark/Increased Contrast 微调以配合 Liquid Glass 并改善色相区分。
findings.source_title: Get to know the new design system — WWDC25 Session 356
findings.notes: concentric 是本次设计核心几何原则;工具栏项按功能与频率分组、不要把符号与文字混为一组。

findings.claim: SwiftUI 采用 Liquid Glass 的关键 API:自定义视图用 .glassEffect()(默认胶囊形,支持 .interactive() 与 .tint());多个玻璃元素必须包进 GlassEffectContainer(玻璃无法采样另一块玻璃,不同容器会导致视觉不一致);配合 @Namespace 用 .glassEffectID() 实现形变过渡;工具栏项自动上玻璃并分组,用 ToolbarSpacer(.fixed/.flexible) 分隔。tint 仅用于传达含义,不要纯装饰。
findings.source_title: Build a SwiftUI app with the new design — WWDC25 Session 323 (WWDCNotes)
findings.notes: 第三方转录(WWDCNotes),但 API 名与 Apple 官方一致。含 tabViewBottomAccessory / .backgroundExtensionEffect / .scrollEdgeEffectStyle。

findings.claim: macOS/AppKit 采用 Liquid Glass:用 NSGlassEffectView(设 contentView 让系统自动保证可读,可调 cornerRadius/tintColor),多块相邻玻璃用 NSGlassEffectContainerView 分组以避免视觉伪影并提升性能;工具栏与侧边栏改为浮在内容之上的玻璃面板,旧的 NSVisualEffectView 侧栏可移除,必要时用 NSBackgroundExtensionView 让内容延伸到边缘。
findings.source_title: Build an AppKit app with the new design — WWDC25 Session 310 (WWDCNotes)
findings.notes: MAformac 若走纯 SwiftUI 可不碰这些,但若需 AppKit 桥接工具栏时相关。

findings.claim: Liquid Glass 的无障碍适配对标准组件是自动的:Reduce Transparency 会让玻璃更磨砂、遮住更多背景;Increased Contrast 让元素基本变纯黑/白并加对比边框;Reduce Motion 降低特效强度并禁用弹性属性。Apple 与独立审计共识:模糊后文本/背景对比应达 4.5:1,关闭透明时提示/控件需在纯色背景上仍可见——发布前必须在这些开关下测试。
findings.source_title: Adopting Liquid Glass — Apple Developer Documentation
findings.notes: Apple 官方页为 SPA 未能直接抓正文;无障碍开关行为由 Session 219 确认(已 fetch),4.5:1 数字来自搜索摘要引第三方审计,需注意非 Apple 原话。

findings.claim: Apple Design Resources 已为新设计更新 Figma/Sketch 模板;2026-06-23 又发布 iOS/iPadOS/macOS 27 版设计套件,含 Liquid Glass 更新、组件与状态扩展、命名向代码对齐、更好的缩放,并为 macOS 新增 Dark Mode。官方模板下载入口:developer.apple.com/design/resources。
findings.source_title: Design kits for iOS, iPadOS, and macOS 27 are here — Apple Developer News (2026-06-23)
findings.notes: 说明 Apple 每年随新系统更新官方 Figma/Sketch 库;macOS 设计模板要用最新版。

findings.claim: Icon Composer 是 Apple 新工具,从单一设计生成 iPhone/iPad/Mac/Apple Watch 通用的分层 Liquid Glass 图标:导入图层后可调 specular highlights、refraction、translucency、shadows;默认 1 层,最多 4 层(Apple 认为这是图标视觉复杂度的合理上界);外观模式重命名为 default/dark/mono;系统自动上圆角矩形遮罩,推到角落的元素有被裁风险。
findings.notes: 细节交叉自 WWDC25 Session 361 与 newsroom(已 fetch);对 MAformac 若要做演示 app 图标相关。

findings.claim: macOS 平台设计取向(HIG designing-for-macOS):Mac 强调指针+键盘、允许高密度信息展示、应充分使用菜单栏/工具栏/键盘快捷键、窗口可精确缩放与全屏;工具栏应去掉自定义背景色,靠布局与分组表达层级而非装饰,按功能与使用频率组织栏项,用 tint 让主操作突出。
findings.source_title: Designing for macOS — Apple Human Interface Guidelines
findings.notes: HIG 长期 macOS 原则,搜索摘要确认;对 MAformac 桌面演示界面的菜单/工具栏/快捷键布局有指导意义。

findings.claim: 内容感知(content-aware)是新系统的功能取向:界面随内容与用户操作实时调整,导航与控件在需要时支持交互、不需要时保持不显眼(unobtrusive);相关操作被智能分组以形成更引导式的体验;材质与动效服务于功能(表达深度、反馈、上下文变化)而非装饰。Action Sheet 现在从触发操作本身弹出而非固定屏幕底部。
findings.source_title: Get to know the new design system — WWDC25 Session 356
findings.notes: 原句 'support interaction where needed, and remain unobtrusive when it is not';对 demo 的即时反应/不抢焦点诉求契合。

content.content: Structured output provided successfully
